title: Quiz: VQE Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of the Variational Quantum Eigensolver

questions.question: What does the variational principle guarantee about E(θ) = ⟨ψ(θ)|Ĥ|ψ(θ)⟩?

options.text: E(θ) can be less than E₀ with good optimization

questions.explanation: The variational principle states that the expectation value of the Hamiltonian for any trial state is always greater than or equal to the true ground state energy E₀.

questions.question: What is the role of the quantum computer in VQE?

options.text: Prepare states and measure expectation values

questions.explanation: The quantum computer prepares the parameterized state |ψ(θ)⟩ and measures Pauli expectation values. The classical computer handles optimization and data management.

questions.question: How is the energy computed from measurements in VQE?

options.text: E = Σₖ wₖ⟨Pₖ⟩ (weighted sum of Pauli expectations)

questions.explanation: The Hamiltonian is decomposed into Pauli strings: H = Σₖ wₖPₖ. Energy is computed as the weighted sum of measured Pauli expectations: E = Σₖ wₖ⟨Pₖ⟩.

questions.question: What is the parameter-shift rule used for?

options.text: Computing exact gradients on quantum hardware

questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule allows computing exact gradients of the cost function: ∂E/∂θ = [E(θ+π/2) - E(θ-π/2)]/2. This requires two circuit evaluations per parameter.

questions.question: VQE requires full error correction to work.
questions.explanation: False. VQE is designed for NISQ (Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum) devices. The variational principle provides some resilience to noise, though error mitigation helps improve results.

questions.question: Why is shot noise proportional to 1/√N_shots?

options.text: Due to central limit theorem for sampling

questions.explanation: Shot noise follows from the central limit theorem: when averaging N independent measurements, the standard error scales as 1/√N. This is a statistical effect, not quantum.

questions.question: What is the purpose of grouping commuting Pauli operators?

options.text: To reduce the number of qubits needed
To reduce the number of circuit evaluations

questions.explanation: Commuting Pauli operators can be measured simultaneously with the same circuit. This reduces the total number of circuit evaluations needed to estimate the energy.

questions.question: Which are sources of error in VQE? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Shot noise, gate errors, and ansatz limitations are major VQE error sources. Nuclear motion is handled by the Born-Oppenheimer approximation before VQE, not during.
